title: Low-sodium French shrimp Pilaf
description: A French-inspired dish featuring saeujeot and shrimp paste, cooked in a bain-marie for a low-sodium and flavorful pilaf.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together saeujeot and 1 tsp water to create a paste.
In a large saucepan, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add brown rice and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add low-sodium chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and create a bain-marie by placing the saucepan in a larger pot with water at a simmer.
Cover the saucepan with a tight-fitting lid and cook for 18-20 minutes, or until the rice is tender.
While the pilaf is cooking, melt unsalted butter in a small saucepan over low heat.
Add cooked shrimp to the melted butter and stir to coat.
Once the pilaf is cooked, fluff it with a fork and stir in the shrimp mixture.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
